prose:
Frontier-orbitals calculation (HOMO/LUMO energies and gap) for ethylene

Method provenance

• Property: HOMO–LUMO energy gap (Koopmans orbital gap) in electron-volts
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density-fitting (RI) in PySCF
• Geometry provenance: single-point on the fixed Cartesian coordinates supplied in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz (no re-optimization; DFT//input-xyz)
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet)
• Software: PySCF (via chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill)
• SCF convergence: converged in 6 cycles
• Engine warnings: no warnings reported
• Live .out log was streamed during the run to a timestamped file `frontier-orbitals_<timestamp>.out` in the working directory; it contains full SCF and orbital information.

Result (gas phase, B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input-xyz)
HOMO energy  = –7.6155818 eV
LUMO energy  = –0.17986527 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap = 7.4357165 eV

These values are taken directly from the chemkit result JSON (field `homo_lumo_gap_eV`). The calculation used the chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill with explicit settings: --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard, in the gas phase.
